name: John Scofield
abstract: John Scofield (born December 26, 1951) is an American guitarist and composer. His music blends jazz, jazz fusion, funk, blues, soul and rock.

concerts.label: Gerry Mulligan, Chet Baker and Stan Getz

works.t: Babes in Arms: My Funny Valentine
There Will Never Be Another You

concerts.label: John Scofield Trio and The Scohorns

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.evidence: I did a tour with John Scofield in 1978 that was my first European tour
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I think I told you I did a duet… It was his concert someplace upstate.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pigeons Playing Ping Pong- Jeremy Schon & Greg Ormont
about_quotes.evidence: we all listen to a lot of instrumental music, especially like kind of groove-oriented instrumental music you know like people like John Scofield more in the jazz world.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pigeons Playing Ping Pong- Jeremy Schon & Greg Ormont interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 8, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: We also played together on Herbie’s New Standards, that was a great recording which featured Michael Brecker, John Scofield, Dave Holland, myself and Don Alias.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWEE: Anyway, I (unintelligible) he and Larry on a complete different style. Really accomplished players.

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: That was in 2006 where you had John Scofield, Bela Fleck, Marc . . . Right, right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
